description: Each step puts 'paloi' against the ground: the foot is the terminal part of the leg that supports standing and movement. 'tolae' (leg) is the limb above it; a shoe or path remains a separate object despite its close relation to the foot.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into open central unrounded 'a'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and draw the tongue back into stressed middle-height rounded 'o'. Raise the tongue into high-front unrounded 'i' for the separate final syllable.
usage_notes: 'paloi' denotes no unit of length. An English measurement in feet stays in a stated source unit or is converted explicitly.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Walking meditation can attend to the foot at each contact with the ground, one ordinary body part carrying the practice.

semantic_domains.physical: the terminal part of the leg that supports standing and walking
